business_problem: Commercial property large-loss reserves are set by adjusters using a mix of prior-loss comparables and gut feel. Reserve adequacy is inconsistent across branches, leading to quarterly reserve adjustments that surprise finance. The CFO has asked for a more defensible reserving aid.
proposed_solution: Build a reserving-aid regression model that predicts ultimate loss for open commercial property claims with estimated damage above $250k. The model output is advisory — adjusters retain the reserve decision. Back-test against 5 years of closed claims.

model_solution.target_definition: Final paid loss + ALAE at claim closure for commercial property claims with initial damage estimate above $250,000.

estimated_value.narrative: Better-calibrated reserves reduce the magnitude of quarterly adjustments and improve finance predictability. Not a direct P&L lever, but meaningfully reduces surprise.
assumptions: Reserving aid is advisory only — no automated posting.
Adjuster adoption is tracked via an override-rate metric.

governance.cycle_time_rationale: Reserves on large commercial claims move slowly; the book the model is trained on refreshes annually.
governance.risk_tier_rationale: Material financial exposure ($250k+ reserves), but humans make every decision and the model is advisory. High but not critical.
